name: Isla Urbana Chaac Kit
description: The Isla Urbana Chaac Kit is a rural-use rainwater harvesting system.

taxonomy.sub_category: Isla Urbana Chaac Kit Updated on January 30, 2024 · Created on October 1, 2020 Isla Urbana Chaac Kit The Isla Urbana Chaac Kit is a rural-use rainwater harvesting system. Developed By IslaUrbana Tested By Isla Urbana Chaac Kit Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Isla Urbana Chaac Kit is a rainwater harvesting and purification system designed as a kit for on-site installation. In addition to capturing rainwater for potable consumption, the system also disinfects and purifies the water for storage. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $408.00 Market Suggested Retail Price (Secondary Currency) 8,900 MXN Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations Area Akvo S.A. de C.V., Inverso, Captacion Muunyal A.C., Ecotec-IK, Ciclo Water, 345 Ingenieria S.A. de C.V., MABA water, Asesores en Gestion Urbana del Agua S.A de C.V., Grup Constructor CYPRO SA de CV Countries Mexico Manufacturing/Building Method This product is made in Mexico by Isla Urbana. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model Users can obtain the product directly from Isla Urbana or from a variety of distributors throughout Mexico. Distributions to Date Status By 2020, 550 had been distributed. Description of the combined methods macroscopic filtration, chlorination, micro-filtration Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 40 L/hr Bacteria reduction Log 6 Virus reduction Log 6 Protozoa reduction Log 6 Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction Log 6 Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Safe water storage capacity (L) Depends on the size of storage tank Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) Unknown Consumables Chlorine, micro-filters Design Specifications The Isla Urbana Chaac Kit is composed of 8 different parts. Rainwater collected from the gutter attached to the roof of the structure is collected into a pipe system where it first passes through a first flush separator to collect dirty water and debris. After this, water passes through a leaf filter for large debris and enters into the on-site storage tank where its momentum is reduced through a turbulence reducer. Next, a chlorine doser and float switch disinfect the water while notifying the user when the tank reaches a certain level. Then, a manual hand pump pushes the water from the tank into a small sediment filter and finally a passive water purification tank where the water is then ready to drink. Product Schematics Technical Support Provided by Isla Urbana for purchases of systems in quantities greater than five. Replacement Components Replaceable components include the chlorine system and the micro-filtration filters. Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Unknown Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety No known safety hazards are related to this product.
